key_points: Transfer students not guaranteed housing; off-campus options fill up fast
Housing assignments delayed due to electrical fire; students left scrambling
Bathroom type (communal vs. suite) unknown until very close to move-in
key_quotes: august 1st was already way too late now i have 15 days to figure out where im living
i went through this last year and you only find out building not room, which also impacts what kind of bathroom you have
Regular housing was first come first serve for me, I got to pick my exact dorm hall, room, and floor

key_points: Notorious suitcase school — campus visibly empties Friday through Sunday
Clubs and involvement fairs are the main way to meet people
Campus comes alive in warm weather; dead in winter months
key_quotes: it is a suitcase school so campus is pretty empty friday to sunday
I haven't seen the chairs this filled in forever, like dang all y'all was hiding in ur dorms
it can be a bit clique-y, and there are people who just go home on weekends

key_points: Multiple food poisoning reports from West Village Commons and Glen dining hall
Dining plan is expensive and mandatory for on-campus students
Halal cart near arts building and Newell mac-and-cheese praised; dining halls generally considered mid
key_quotes: Getting food poisoning at Towson is a rite of passage. We all went through it.
The dining plan at Towson was soo expensive. I was just a sheep and never really thought about what they were charging for.
my girlfriend, who hasn't thrown up since probably 6th grade, told me this morning how she was throwing up at 4:00 AM from food poisoning

key_points: Counseling center exists but students report it failing them in crisis situations
Staff genuinely express care, but resources don't match student need
Comfort dog only available during finals/midterms, not year-round
key_quotes: counseling center didn't help me and just... the university doesn't have the resources to help me unfortunately
I wish students would ask for help more often. We care about you, deeply.
Wish we had a comfort dog available more frequently in like the counseling center or smth

key_points: Financial aid office communication is slow and frustrating for students
University billed a graduate student nearly $1,000 three months after degree was conferred
In-state tuition and merit scholarships make it a strong value for Maryland residents
key_quotes: I'm being charged for a tuition billing error three months after degree was conferred
im going to Towson for pretty much free since im in state
I emailed the Financial Aid office about when we were going to receive our aid packages and I have gotten a response — apparently the processing system is back up

key_points: STEM, forensic chem, and EMF programs receive specific praise from students
Some professors post student emails publicly on Facebook — possible FERPA violation
Core curriculum requirements can blindside transfer students and delay graduation
key_quotes: The professors care, ofc there's bad ones mixed in but that's everywhere
The admin has made it impossible to graduate on time. Just learned that the Core 13 class wasn't really Core 13
There is a particular professor at TU who shares student emails on FB... it's still unnerving to see a private communication shared publicly

key_points: Enrollment office gives wrong information; multiple students describe weeks of back-and-forth
Snow day and emergency decisions consistently called too late, angering commuters
Parking permit costs ~$220 but garages regularly blocked off with no refund discussion
key_quotes: I have been trying to sort out my enrollment situation since the 13th. Two weeks of back and forth with admissions. First the student staff gave me completely wrong information
my biggest mistake was going to TU and it's only getting worse. i'd recommend a new school or community college than towson. place is ran by the rats.
People pay like $220 for a permit just not to park — is ridiculous

key_points: Baltimore is 15-20 minutes away, not 30 — easy access to a real city
Campus is walkable and pretty; warm weather transforms outdoor spaces
Driving and parking are chaotic — speeding in garages, dangerous crosswalks, chronic traffic jams
key_quotes: Baltimore, depending on where you're going, is not 30 mins away. Mostly 15-20.
The majority of people on campus drive like assholes. Ignoring stop signs, speeding through crosswalks when pedestrians are actively there.
theres alot to do, the campus size is pretty manageable while still having good programs, uptown is super close with walking distance

key_points: EMF and mass comm programs have dedicated alumni but field is competitive
Handshake app criticized as unhelpful for finding on-campus jobs
Towson is a Center of Actuarial Excellence — niche but real credential for that field
key_quotes: I literally do not do anything with that major anymore - ha!
Handshake isn't helpful and only shows jobs that I'm not even qualified for
Towson really emphasizes students being able to do research opportunities

key_points: Excellent value for Maryland in-state students, especially with merit aid
Mandatory meal plans add significant cost with questionable food quality payoff
Out-of-state cost (~$34K) competitive with some peers but needs scholarship to justify
key_quotes: im going to Towson for pretty much free since im in state
The dining plan at Towson was soo expensive. I was just a sheep
she received a generous merit scholarship which actually makes it cost-competitive to our in-state tuition at UMASS-Amherst

key_points: Warm, diverse community when people are around — but weekends feel hollow
Transfers and commuters report feeling disconnected; cliques form quickly
Students who engage with clubs and campus events consistently report loving it
key_quotes: Towson is way better than it gets credit for
I transferred to Towson as well and I love it here so much!
My experience has been complete opposite... the admin has made it impossible to graduate on time

key_points: **Safety**: Shooting in dorm, fatal shooting near campus circle, bomb threat at Union all within recent memory
**Administration**: Students billed billing errors post-graduation, given wrong enrollment info, and penalized for university mistakes
**Food Safety**: Recurring food poisoning reports from multiple dining halls described as a campus 'rite of passage'
key_quotes: Shooting at Towson University dorm leaves 1 hurt
They didn't even cancel classes during the bomb threat last semester
It's 100% believable, my freshmen year I got massive food poisoning from Newell dining hall my first week, and since then I have one GI issue after another

key_points: Halal cart near the arts/Glen building is beloved and cheap — skip the Union
Bus route system is more navigable than it looks; free Purple Loop shuttle to mall area
Grade data by professor publicly available at myclassgrades.com — use it before registering
key_quotes: the halal cart outside at Glen is amazing i really recommend going there
I've been here for almost ten years... I spent a LOT of time figuring out the easiest/quickest way to get places
I pulled the official grade data for Towson so you can actually see how hard a professor grades before you pick your classes
